behavior_summary: LAMP3::look: When a player/actor examines the wall lamp, the script composes the literal string 'wall lamp ' and invokes the item's bark/display routine so the description is shown to the viewer.

LAMP3::use: When the lamp is used, the script reads the lamp's frame/state and spawns/activates a nearby effect via FREE::2411. The effect id and which coordinate is offset by +0x20 depend on the lamp's frame (frame==0 uses effect id 0x013A and offsets X; otherwise uses effect id 0x013D and offsets Y).
notable_control_flow: LAMP3::look allocates a temporary string, converts it to a pointer, pushes the viewer/item arguments and calls Item::bark, then frees the temporary string before returning.

LAMP3::use queries Item::getFrame and branches: one branch for frame == 0, the other for frame != 0; both branches fetch Z, Y, X via Item::getZ/getY/getX, add 0x20 to a different coordinate depending on the branch, then call FREE::2411 with the computed coordinates, a zero byte flag, a frame-dependent effect id, and the item reference.
No global flags are observed to be read or written by these handlers; behavior is local to the item and effect spawn routine.
open_questions: What exactly does FREE::2411 implement? It appears to spawn or toggle a visual/lighting effect (inferred from arguments and differing effect ids), but the precise effect semantics are unknown.
What do the two effect ids (0x013A and 0x013D) represent relative to lamp frames (e.g., light on vs. light off, or two orientation-specific visuals)?
Why is a +0x20 offset applied to X in one branch and to Y in the other? This likely aligns the spawned effect to a lamp sprite orientation, but exact anchor semantics are not confirmed.
Are there other events (e.g., enterFastArea/leaveFastArea) implemented elsewhere for this lamp that change frame or global state?

main_function.name: U8P_LAMP3::look
main_function.purpose: Display a short descriptive text for the wall lamp to the viewer by calling the item's bark/display routine with the literal string 'wall lamp '.
entry_conditions: Event 0: invoked when the Avatar (or relevant actor) 'looks at' or examines this lamp (examination/inspect trigger).

function_map.name: U8P_LAMP3::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_LAMP3::look' (examine) event; compose and pass the description string to Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_LAMP3::use
function_map.role: Handle the 'use' event: read lamp frame/state, compute an offset coordinate and call FREE::2411 to spawn/trigger a frame-dependent effect near the lamp.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine invoked to display a short text or message for an item to a player/process.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Return the item's current frame/state; drives branch in LAMP3::use.

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Return item X coordinate (used by LAMP3::use and subject to an offset in one branch).

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Return item Y coordinate (used by LAMP3::use and subject to an offset in the alternate branch).

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Return item Z/level coordinate (passed unchanged to FREE::2411).

function_map.name: FREE::2411
function_map.role: Engine/system routine called to spawn/trigger an effect at computed coordinates with an effect id; exact behavior inferred (likely visual/lighting effect).

external_calls.purpose: Display the composed description string 'wall lamp ' to the viewer/process.
Obtain the lamp's frame/state to decide which effect id to use and which coordinate to offset.
Fetch item's Z coordinate to pass to the effect spawn routine.
Fetch item's Y coordinate; in the branch frame != 0 this Y value is incremented by 0x20 before calling FREE::2411.
Fetch item's X coordinate; in the branch frame == 0 this X value is incremented by 0x20 before calling FREE::2411.
Spawn or trigger a frame-dependent effect at computed coordinates, passing a zero byte flag, a chosen effect id (0x013A or 0x013D), and the item reference.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: LAMP3::look: When a player/actor examines the wall lamp, the script composes the literal string 'wall lamp ' and invokes the item's bark/display routine so the description is shown to the viewer.
